title: chunks — Postgres + pgvector table contract
description: Authoritative shape of the `chunks` table the pipeline must produce. CI compares table columns and Postgres types against this contract exactly. The vector dimension is non-negotiable because it is dictated by the pinned embedding model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 → 384).

columns.name: id
columns.description: Surrogate chunk id. BIGSERIAL or GENERATED ALWAYS AS IDENTITY both acceptable.

columns.name: doc_id
columns.description: Foreign key back to the source document (1..5000 in the synthetic corpus).

columns.name: chunk_text
columns.description: Literal text content of the chunk. MUST preserve memo codes verbatim — that is how the golden eval set finds its targets.

columns.name: embedding
columns.description: Embedding produced by sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2 (384 dims). Vectors MUST be L2-normalised so cosine and inner-product searches give equivalent rankings.

columns.name: tokens
columns.description: Approximate token count of chunk_text. Cheap heuristic acceptable (word count, or tokenizer-based) — used as a guard against runaway chunks.

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: Insert timestamp. DEFAULT now() is acceptable. Used for observability, not for retrieval logic.

index.description: An ANN index on `embedding` is recommended for realistic latency. The rubric does NOT enforce its presence (recall@5 on 5k docs is fast even with a sequential scan), but it is the difference between a portfolio piece and a real production pipeline.
